title: myrtus communis subsp. tarentina
intro: Myrtus communis subsp. tarentina, also known as the Calabrian myrtle, is a subspecies of the myrtle plant, a small, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Myrtus communis subsp. tarentina include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include essential oils, teas, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it's also used as an ingredient in traditional remedies and cosmetics.
